Very high disruptor personality, anti-censorship orientation, and strong populist anti-elitism strongly favor minimal content moderation as a check against perceived elite control of speech.
Very low anti-censorship orientation and high authoritarianism strongly oppose freedom from moderation, while high agreeableness and trust in institutions reinforce support for moderation.
